issues: The draft describes this as a Huskies article with no Sockeyes connection, but the OCR contains 'Czenczek, UBC' — Stan Czenczek is a known 1987 Sockeyes player appearing as a UBC player in these game records, making this tangentially relevant to the archive.

The draft's long description says 'Huskies defeated UBC 8-1 and Saskatchewan 5-3' — the second game score is wrong; the OCR shows Saskatchewan defeated UBC in two games (8-1 on Saturday and an earlier game), not a 'Saskatchewan 5-3' result.
The headline 'Huskies Scoring' is a section label within the article, not the main headline. The actual game heading visible in the OCR is 'Game Saturday Saskatchewan 8 UBC 1'.
The long description does not mention Czenczek (former Sockeyes player) appearing in the game records, which is the primary reason this scan is archive-relevant.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Box scores and statistics from two University of Saskatchewan Huskies vs. UBC hockey games (November 1987), featuring former Richmond Sockeyes player Stan Czenczek listed as a UBC skater.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Star-Phoenix (Saskatoon) page 25 carries detailed box scores for two Saskatchewan Huskies vs. UBC men's hockey games. In the Saturday game Saskatchewan defeated UBC 8-1, with goal scorers including Morrison, Lloyd, Lemire, Leier, Lovsin, Williams, and Sakundiak. A second game is also partially reported. Former Richmond Sockeyes player Stan Czenczek appears in the penalty summaries as a UBC player, providing the archival link to the 1987 Sockeyes. Season scoring leaders for the Huskies are also listed, with Morrison at 21 goals and 35 points.
review.notes: This scan is tangentially relevant to the Sockeyes archive because ex-Sockeye Stan Czenczek (also known as "Chenner") appears as a UBC player in the penalty records of both games. The draft missed this connection entirely and also misstated the second game's score. Editor should confirm whether Czenczek's post-Sockeyes UBC career warrants full archival inclusion.
